thag16.4:1.2: a heap of festering wounds, shored up:
thag16.4:1.3: ill, but the object of many resolves,
thag16.4:1.4: where there is nothing lasting or sure.
thag16.4:3.4: but not a seeker for the further shore.
thag16.4:4.4: but not a seeker for the further shore.
thag16.4:5.1: Like a newly painted unguent pot—
thag16.4:5.4: but not a seeker for the further shore.
thag16.4:6.1: The hunter set out the snares,
thag16.4:6.2: but the deer didn’t go near the trap.
thag16.4:6.3: Having eaten the bait, we go,
thag16.4:7.2: the deer didn’t go near the trap.
thag16.4:7.3: Having eaten the bait, we go,
thag16.4:8.1: I see in the world people with wealth
thag16.4:8.2: who, from delusion, don’t make a gift of the treasure they’ve gained.
thag16.4:8.4: hoping for even more sensual pleasures.
thag16.4:9.1: A king who, by force, has conquered the world
thag16.4:9.2: and rules over the earth to the edge of the sea,
thag16.4:9.3: dissatisfied with the ocean’s near shore,
thag16.4:9.4: longs for the ocean’s far shore as well.
thag16.4:10.2: go to death with craving unabated.
thag16.4:10.3: Unsated, they leave the body behind,
thag16.4:10.4: having not had enough of the world’s sensual pleasures.
thag16.4:11.1: One’s relatives weep & pull out their hair.
thag16.4:11.2: ‘Oh woe, our loved one is dead,’ they cry.
thag16.4:11.3: Carrying him off, wrapped in a piece of cloth,
thag16.4:11.4: they place him on a pyre, then set him on fire.
thag16.4:12.1: So he burns, poked with sticks,
thag16.4:12.2: in just one piece of cloth, leaving all his possessions behind.
thag16.4:12.3: They are not shelters for one who has died—
thag16.4:13.2: while the being goes on, in line with his kamma.
thag16.4:13.3: No wealth at all follows the dead one—
thag16.4:13.4: not children, wives, dominion, or riches.
thag16.4:14.1: Long life can’t be gotten with wealth,
thag16.4:14.2: nor aging warded off with treasure.
thag16.4:14.3: The wise say this life is next to nothing—
thag16.4:15.1: The rich & the poor touch the touch of Death.
thag16.4:15.2: The foolish & wise are touched by it, too.
thag16.4:15.3: But while fools lie as if slain by their folly,
thag16.4:15.4: the wise don’t tremble when touched by the touch.
thag16.4:16.2: one attains to mastery, is better than wealth—
thag16.4:16.3: for those who haven’t reached mastery go from existence to existence,
thag16.4:16.4: out of delusion, doing bad deeds.
thag16.4:17.1: One goes to a womb & to the next world,
thag16.4:17.2: falling into the wandering on —one thing after another—
thag16.4:17.3: while those of weak discernment, trusting in one,
thag16.4:17.4: also go to a womb & to the next world.
thag16.4:18.1: Just as an evil thief caught at the break-in
thag16.4:18.2: is destroyed by his own act,
thag16.4:18.3: so evil people —after dying, in the next world—
thag16.4:18.4: are destroyed by their own acts.
thag16.4:19.2: in various ways disturb the mind.
thag16.4:19.3: Seeing the drawbacks in sensual objects:
thag16.4:19.4: that’s why, O king, I went forth.
thag16.4:20.2: —young & old— at the break-up of the body.
thag16.4:20.3: Knowing this, O king, I went forth.
thag16.4:20.4: The contemplative life is better for sure. * * *
thag16.4:21.1: Out of conviction, I went forth
thag16.4:22.3: pain in the entry into the womb
thag16.4:23.1: seeing this peril, I was then dismayed—
thag16.4:23.4: on attaining the end of the effluents.
thag16.4:24.1: The Teacher has been served by me;
thag16.4:25.1: And the goal for which I went forth
